text: OSINTdefender From this post it’s pretty clear that tonight’s agreement isn’t just a two-week ceasefire in the eyes of President Trump, but an end to the Iran War. U.S. President Donald J. Trump calls Wednesday a “big day for World Peace!”, following tonight’s announced two-week ceasefire agreement with Iran. Trump states that the United States will assist with the buildup of ships in the Strait of Hormuz and that reconstruction can begin in Iran. The President adds that, “We'll be loading up with supplies of all kinds, and just ‘hangin' around’ in order to make sure that everything goes well. I feel confident that it will. Just like we are experiencing in the U.S., this could be the Golden Age of the Middle East!!!” - OSINTdefender tweet

OSINTdefender During a brief call tonight with the AFP, President Trump stated that the United States has achieved, “Total and complete victory. 100 percent. No question about it,” in the Iran War, following the announced two-week ceasefire with Iran. Trump added that he believes China persuaded Iran to make a deal as the deadline approached Tuesday, and that the country’s stockpile of highly-enriched uranium will be “perfectly taken care of,” under the ceasefire agreement between Iran and the United States. tweet

↩️ Reply to Middle East Spectator — MES: "Regarding the Israeli attack on a car in Lebanon; this is unfortunately part of ‘ordinary’ attacks (albeit illegal and a violation) that were already happening during the ceasefire in Lebanon which went into effect in 2024.I wouldn’t expect Israel to abstain from striking some targets inside Lebanon completely, they didn’t abide by that during the previous ceasefire and they likely won’t be abiding by that now.What will give an indication whether Israel is ‘complying’ with the current ceasefire (in a relative sense) is if they stop their advance in the villages, stop the the bombing of major urban centers most importantly Beirut, and the assassination of senior staff." I don't agree If the result of this war to take us back to what was before, it would not have been worth it to join a war to lose 1000 of our best men and lose thousands of houses across Lebanon. That would be a new loss. A humiliating one. The ceasefire either includes Lebanon and enforces adherence to the 2024 deal that ended the war or else Iran accepted a ceasefire from weak position and was not able to enforce anything

🇮🇶 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 American Journalist Shelly Kittleson Released After One Week Kataib Hezbollah (Islamic Resistance in Iraq) released 49-year-old American journalist Shelly Kittleson after holding her for one week. Kittleson is a reporter specializing in the West Asia, with over a decade covering Iraq, Syria, and Afghanistan. She has contributed to Al-Monitor, Foreign Policy, and Politico. Abu Mujahid al-Assaf announced the decision and posted a video of Kittleson recorded during her time in custody, in which she discussed her activities and information-gathering in Iraq. Conditions: She must leave Iraq immediately. The release included a quiet prisoner swap with detained resistance members. The resistance group called it a one-time goodwill gesture amid war with the Zionist-American enemy.

🇨🇳 🇮🇷 According to Associated Press , the two-week ceasefire between Washington and Tehran was made possible in part due to China’s involvement. As Iran’s largest trading partner, Beijing reportedly encouraged Tehran throughout negotiations to pursue a ceasefire path. AP cites two anonymous officials saying China “urged Iranians to find a way to a ceasefire as talks progressed.” The report adds that Beijing worked through intermediaries—Pakistan, Turkey, and Egypt—to help facilitate a diplomatic breakthrough. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

WarMonitor The Strait of Hormuz was effectively a free passageway before the war. Now Iran is deciding who gets through and making ships buy tickets to do it, boosting its leverage over global energy, while funding its military and proxies in the process. The NYT is reporting that Iran, in addition to gaining administrative control over the Strait of Hormuz, will now charge $2m per ship for safe passage, which it will split with Oman. It was free pre-war. Trump & Netanyahu have increased Iranian leverage over the global economy. - Adam Schwarz tweet
